turns.user_message: I used to brush off little mistakes without a second thought, but lately everything just sticks to me. It’s strange how a simple misstep now feels like proof that I’m fundamentally lacking. I catch myself replaying minor moments over and over, wondering why I can’t just let them go like I always did before. The weight of it sits heavy in my chest, making even quiet days feel exhausting. I keep telling myself it’s just a rough patch, but the doubt lingers longer than it ever used to.

It’s frustrating because I used to navigate these moments with a quiet confidence, but now I feel completely hollowed out by my own expectations. Every small setback just piles up, turning into this constant background noise that tells me I’m not enough. I try to shake it off the way I always did, but the old resilience just isn’t there anymore. Instead, I find myself shrinking back, convinced that I’m falling short in ways I never noticed before. It’s exhausting to carry this heavy sense of inadequacy around when I know I used to handle things so much lighter.

I honestly don’t recognize myself anymore because I used to walk through life feeling grounded, and now I’m just bracing for the moment I’m found out. This creeping sense of worthlessness has completely taken over, twisting every passing thought into another reminder of how far I’ve fallen. I keep waiting for that old spark to return, but it’s just been swallowed by this relentless inner critic that won’t let up. It feels like I’m permanently stuck in a loop of proving I don’t measure up, no matter what I try. The contrast between who I was and who I am now is so sharp it actually aches, leaving me completely drained.

generation_metadata.diversity_anchor: The speaker opens by contrasting with how things used to be. This used to not bother the speaker.
